content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Daniel Robertson, Esq. 
655 University Avenue, Suite 150 
Sacramento, CA 95825 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-16-119 
Dear Mr. Robertson: 
September 1,2016 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act") 1 and Section 1090. Please note that we do not advise on any 
other area of law, including Public Contract Code or common law conflicts of interest. W c are also 
not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby ( 1975) I FPPC Ops. 71 ), and any advice 
we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and Section I 090, not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law 
conflict of interest. 
In regard to our advice on Section I 090, wc arc required to forward your request and all 
pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the Los Angeles County 
District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097. l(c)(3).) We did not receive a 
written response from either entity. (Section 1097. l(c)(4).) We are also required to advise you that, 
for purposes of Section l 090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding 
against any individual other than the requcstor." (See Section 1097.1 (c)(5).) 
QUESTION 
May the Executive Director of the Girls Athletic Leadership Schools Los Angeles 
("GALS") donate the license to use curriculum she has created to GALS? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. Neither the Act nor Section 1090 prohibit the Executive Director of GALS from 
donating the license to use curriculum she has created to GALS because she has no financial 
interest involved in the decision. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 1 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 1. Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


FACTS 
File No. A-16-119 
Page No. 2 
Your office represents GALS as legal counsel. You are writing on behalf of GALS and 
Carrie Wagner, the Executive Director of GALS, to request fonnal advice regarding whether 
Ms. Wagner may donate a license to GALS to use curriculum owned by Ms. Wagner. 
GALS is a California nonprofit public benefit corporation that operates Girls Athletic 
Leadership School, a California public charter school. GALS operates the charter school pursuant to 
a charter petition hrranted by the Los Angeles Unified School District. The 2016-2017 school year 
will be the first year of operation of the charter school. The charter school serves students in grades 
6-8. Ms. Wagner will be responsible for implementing the policies and budget adopted by the 
governing board of GALS. She will further be responsible for overseeing the day-to-day affairs of 
GALS and supervising staff among other responsibilities. 
Ms. Wagner owns certain curriculum developed by her in her personal capacity. The value 
of the curriculum is unknown. Likewise, the value of a license to use the curriculum is unknown. 
To date, Ms. Wagner has not granted use of the curriculum to any party, either as a donation or in 
exchange for payment. 
Pursuant to a proposed agreement, Ms. Wagner proposes to retain ownership of the 
curriculum while donating use of her curriculum to GALS at no cost to GALS. Specifically, 
Ms. Wagner proposes to grant a non-exclusive, non-revocable, perpetual license to GALS to allow 
GALS to use her curriculum for its educational programs. Under the proposed agreement, GALS 
may duplicate, display, publish, and prepare derivative works of the curriculum for its students. 
However, Ms. Wagner would be pennitted to grant use of the agreement to other entities in the 
future on a non-exclusive basis, either as a donation or in exchange for payment. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or 
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the 
official has a financial interest. The Acf s conflict-of-interest provisions only apply to conflicts of 
interests based on the following: 
• Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth 
S2,000 or more. (Section 87103(a).) 
• Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth $2,000 
or more. (Section 87103(b).) 
• Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in the 
regular course of business on tenns available to the public without regard to official status, 
aghrregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the public official 
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(c).) 


File No. A-16-1 19 
Page No. 3 
• 
Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, 
employee, or holds any position of management. (Section 87103(d).) 
• Any donor of, or any intennediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating $460 or 
more in value provided to, received by, or promised to the public official within 12 months 
prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(e).) 
• 
In addition, a public official's personal finances are deemed to be directly involved in a 
governmental decision that will have any financial effect on his or her personal finances or 
those of his or her immediate family. (Section 87103.) 
Based on the facts provided, it does not appear Executive Director Wagner has any 
economic interests that would be implicated by the governmental decision to use the curriculum she 
developed. In addition, because she will not be receiving any compensation in exchange for 
donating the curriculum to the school, the decision will have no effect on her personal finances. 
Assuming the accuracy of these facts, she will not have an interest in the decision at issue. 
Section 1090 
Section 1090 generally prohibits public officers, while acting in their official capacities, 
from making contracts in which they are financially interested. Section I 090 is concerned with 
financial interests, other than remote or minimal interests, that prevent public officials from 
exercising absolute loyalty and undivided allegiance in furthering the best interests of their 
agencies. (Stigall,•. Tq{r ( 1962) 58 Cal.2d 565, 569.) Section 1090 is intended "not only to strike at 
actual impropriety, but also to strike at the appearance of impropriety." ( Ci-v of Imperial Beach ,,. 
Bailey (1980) 103 Cal.App.3d 191, 197.) 
Under Section 1090, "the prohibited act is the making of a contract in which the official has 
a financial interest." (People, .. Honig (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 289, 333.) A contract that violates 
Section 1090 is void. (Thomson, .. Call (1985) 38 Cal.3d 633, 646.) The prohibition applies 
regardless of whether the tenns of the contract are fair and equitable to all parties. (Id. at pp. 646-
649.) 
Again Executive Director Wagner will not be receiving any compensation for donating the 
curriculum to the school and nothing in the facts you provided suggests that the product's value will 
in any way be enhanced by allowing GALS to use the curriculum for free. Based on this conclusion, 
we do not view the contract as one that will provide any personal benefit to Executive Director 
Wagner. Therefore, Executive Director Wagner does not have a conflict under Section 1090. If 
donating curriculum to the school would somehow result in financial gain to director Wagner please 
seek further assistance.2 You have stated that Executive Director Wagner may decide to sell this 
l The Attorney General has advised that although section 1090 nowhere specifically defines a "financial 
interest," case law and previous Attorney General opinions indicate that forbidden financial interests may be indirect as 
well as direct, and may involve financial losses. or the possibility of losses. as well as the prospect of pecuniary gain. 
(See 86 Ops. Cal Atty. Gen. 138.) 


File No. A-16-119 
Page No. 4 
curriculum in the future. If for some reason using this curriculum at the school would increase sales 
in the future, there may be a conflict. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
SKB:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
By: 
Sukhi K. Brar 
Counsel, Legal Division

QUESTION: May the Executive Director of the Girls Athletic Leadership Schools Los Angeles 
("GALS") donate the license to use curriculum she has created to GALS?

CONCLUSION: Yes. Neither the Act nor Section 1090 prohibit the Executive Director of GALS from 
donating the license to use curriculum she has created to GALS because she has no financial 
interest involved in the decision.All 
regulatory references are to Title 1. Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
